metadata.title: AI Effect Generator - Create Visual Effects from Images
metadata.description: Create AI visual effects from images and prompts. Build cinematic transitions, product reveals, social hooks, and image-to-video concepts in one workspace.

workspace.title: AI Effect Generator
workspace.description: Upload a reference image or describe the scene you want, then choose an effect direction for a polished visual transformation workflow.
workspace.defaultPrompt: Turn this image into a cinematic AI video effect with a smooth camera move, realistic lighting, clear subject focus, and a clean ending frame for social sharing.

uploadSlots.label: Reference image
uploadSlots.description: Upload the portrait, product, landscape, or scene you want to transform.

workspace.previewDescription: Preview the camera move, effect direction, and final visual rhythm.
workspace.readyText: Your effect setup is ready for generation.

showcase.title: AI effects you can build from one workspace
showcase.description: Start with a still image, product shot, portrait, or travel scene and shape the visual transformation with a clear prompt.

items.title: Cinematic Product Reveal
items.description: Turn a static product image into a short ad-style reveal with controlled lighting and movement.
items.prompt: Animate this product image with a slow push-in, premium studio lighting, subtle reflection movement, and a clean branded ending frame.

items.title: Earth Zoom Transition
items.description: Use a location image as the start frame for a dramatic zoom-out visual.
items.prompt: Start from the center of this location image and pull the camera upward into a smooth Earth zoom out ending in an orbital view.

items.title: Portrait Motion Effect
items.description: Add natural motion and atmosphere to a portrait without rebuilding the whole scene.
items.prompt: Animate this portrait with subtle head movement, natural blinking, soft wind in the hair, and realistic cinematic lighting.

items.title: Social Hook Visual
items.description: Create a short vertical motion effect designed for the first seconds of a Reel or TikTok.
items.prompt: Create a fast vertical social video effect with a strong opening camera move, crisp subject focus, and a satisfying final frame.

what.title: AI effects that start from a real creative input
what.description: People searching for an AI effect generator usually want to upload an image or describe a scene, then turn that source into a motion effect they can use for ads, product reveals, social hooks, or storytelling tests.

items.title: Image-to-effect workflows
items.description: Use a product, portrait, location, or campaign image as the source, then describe how the subject, camera, lighting, and environment should change over time.

items.title: Prompt-led visual direction
items.description: Write the effect as a short creative brief with subject motion, camera movement, style, and ending frame instead of manually editing keyframes, masks, and transitions.

items.title: Reusable effect presets
items.description: Start from common directions such as Earth zoom, product reveal, portrait motion, talking character, or social hook, then adjust the prompt for the specific asset.

how.title: How to generate an AI effect
how.description: A useful effect page should show the workflow before the SEO copy: source input, motion direction, generation, and review. That matches how creators actually test visual effects.

steps.title: Upload or describe
steps.description: Add a source image or write a scene description that names the subject, visual style, intended motion, and the final moment you want the viewer to remember.

steps.title: Choose effect settings
steps.description: Choose the effect family and platform format before generating so the output is shaped for a product ad, vertical hook, location reveal, or presentation clip.

steps.title: Generate and refine
steps.description: Review whether the effect keeps the subject recognizable, then refine camera speed, framing, lighting, physics, atmosphere, or ending frame in the next prompt.

features.title: Features that support practical effect production
features.description: The page should explain which controls affect output quality and how creators can use them. That gives the page real value beyond broad claims about AI visual effects.

items.title: Reference image support
items.description: Anchor the effect to a real product, person, place, or brand asset so the generated motion starts from something the creator already needs to use.

items.title: Preset effect direction
items.description: Use proven effect categories to avoid rewriting the same setup from scratch, then add the details that make the result specific to the scene.

items.title: Platform aspect ratios
items.description: Plan effects for vertical social posts, horizontal video, square feeds, landing pages, or ad placements before the frame composition is locked in.

items.title: Prompt refinement
items.description: Iterate on subject movement, camera motion, atmosphere, realism, and final frame clarity instead of treating the first generated clip as the final result.

useCases.title: Best use cases for AI visual effects
useCases.description: AI effects are valuable when they solve a specific creative job: grabbing attention, explaining a product, making a static asset move, or testing a visual concept before a full production pass.

items.title: Short-form social hooks
items.description: Create first-second visuals for TikTok, Reels, Shorts, and paid social ads where motion needs to communicate the idea before the caption or voiceover does.

items.title: Product and ecommerce assets
items.description: Turn static catalog shots into product reveals, rotating hero clips, packaging transitions, seasonal variations, and test ads without reshooting every asset.

items.title: Travel and location videos
items.description: Add motion, zooms, atmosphere, and environmental context to places, venues, destination imagery, real estate photos, or event spaces.

items.title: Creator experiments
items.description: Test several effect directions before committing to a full edit, paid ad concept, higher-cost video generation, or manual motion design workflow.

value.title: Why AI effects need a tool-first page
value.description: Users searching for an AI effect generator want a usable workspace, not only a marketing page. The page earns trust by explaining how inputs, prompts, and review criteria change the result.

points.title: The input comes first
points.description: Putting upload, prompt, and settings above the fold matches the real intent behind effect searches: users want to start from their own source material quickly.

points.title: The page can scale
points.description: New effect pages can reuse the same template while still changing examples, inputs, use cases, and FAQs so the content does not become a thin variable swap.

points.title: The SEO stays specific
points.description: Each effect can answer its own search intent with unique examples, limitations, quality advice, and related tools without duplicating component code.

related.title: Explore specific AI effect workflows
related.description: Use these focused pages when you know the exact effect or model workflow you want to generate.

tools.title: Earth Zoom AI
tools.description: Create a ground-to-space zoom-out effect from a starting image.

tools.title: Hailuo AI Video Generator
tools.description: Generate image-to-video motion with prompt-controlled camera direction.

tools.title: Seedance AI Generator
tools.description: Build character motion, dance clips, and short social video scenes.

tools.title: LipSync Generator
tools.description: Match speech audio to a face video for talking-head and localization workflows.

faq.title: AI Effect Generator FAQ
faq.description: These questions cover the common search intent behind AI effect tools: inputs, output direction, aspect ratios, quality, and commercial use.

items.question: What can I upload to the AI Effect Generator?
items.answer: You can start from a product photo, portrait, location image, artwork, or another visual reference. Clear images with a recognizable subject usually produce cleaner effect direction.

items.question: Do I need to write a long prompt?
items.answer: No. A strong prompt should name the subject, camera movement, visual style, lighting, and desired ending frame. Short but specific prompts usually work better than vague cinematic adjectives.

items.question: Which aspect ratio should I choose?
items.answer: Use 9:16 for TikTok, Reels, and Shorts, 16:9 for YouTube and website video, 1:1 for feed posts, and 4:5 for portrait social placements.

items.question: Can I create Earth zoom effects from this page?
items.answer: Yes. The general effect page includes an Earth zoom preset, while the dedicated Earth Zoom AI page gives that workflow its own examples, SEO sections, and focused FAQ.

items.question: Can I use AI effects for product ads?
items.answer: Yes. Product images are a strong use case because the source frame gives the generator a clear subject, surface, and composition to animate into a reveal or commercial motion test.

items.question: Why does an effect sometimes look different from my prompt?
items.answer: AI video effects are probabilistic. If the result drifts, make the subject, motion, camera direction, and negative constraints more explicit, then generate another variation.

items.question: Will this replace the dedicated model pages?
items.answer: No. This page is a general effect workspace. Dedicated pages like Veo 3, Hailuo, Seedance, LipSync, and Earth Zoom keep their own focused copy, settings, examples, and FAQ.

items.question: Can I use generated effects commercially?
items.answer: Commercial use depends on the source assets, model provider terms, and your plan. Use images you own or have permission to use, and avoid copyrighted characters, logos, or private likenesses without rights.

finalCta.title: Start an AI effect from your own image
finalCta.description: Upload a reference, choose the effect direction, and prepare a visual transformation workflow from the first viewport.
